behavior_summary: When the object receives a 'U8P_EWLAMPTP::look' event, it prepares process attribution/state, constructs the literal text "lamp ", and invokes the item's display/bark routine so the player sees the word "lamp".

The handler runs under a modified process context (set info + process exclude) so the bark is attributed/filtered appropriately, then frees the temporary string, evaluates the bark return value with an implication operation, and suspends/returns.
This behavior is local to the process and the provided actor/item context; no global flags are read or written by this script.
notable_control_flow: Set process info using an indirect pointer and the context argument (BP+06h) to attribute the subsequent bark call.
Apply process exclusion state (process exclude) before calling Item::bark.
Call Item::bark with the literal string "lamp ", the context actor/item, and a PID pushed for process attribution.
Free the temporary string buffer created for the message.
Consume the return value from Item::bark and use an 'implies' operation on it, then pop a temp and suspend/return.
open_questions: Exactly which PID value is pushed before calling Item::bark and whether it represents the item, the viewer, or a helper process (inferred but not explicit).
Precise semantics of the 'process exclude' opcode in this engine: whether it hides the process, changes visibility, or modifies event routing.
Whether the 'implies' operation influences further game state or merely drives the handler's boolean return (its downstream effect is not shown).
Confirm which runtime/free routine is used to release the constructed "lamp " string (the disassembly implies a free but the target symbol is not explicit).

main_function.name: U8P_EWLAMPTP::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to the player's or actor's 'U8P_EWLAMPTP::look' action by presenting the short text description "lamp " via the item's bark/display routine, attributing the action to a specific process/context and cleaning up temporaries.
entry_conditions: Event 0: 'U8P_EWLAMPTP::look' (triggered when the player or an actor examines/looks at this object)

function_map.name: U8P_EWLAMPTP::look
function_map.role: Handle U8P_EWLAMPTP::look/examine event for the lamp object; construct and dispatch the short display string.

function_map.name: U8P_EWLAMPTP::set info
function_map.role: Configure process/object attribution/state prior to calling display routines (used to set caller context).

function_map.name: U8P_EWLAMPTP::process exclude
function_map.role: Modify process exclusion/visibility flags so the subsequent bark runs with the intended filtering/attribution.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Display or emit the provided short text string ('lamp ') to the target viewer/process.

function_map.name: U8P_EWLAMPTP::free
function_map.role: Deallocate the temporary string buffer created for the bark message (inferred).

function_map.name: suspend
function_map.role: End the handler by suspending/returning control to the engine (observed as final opcode).

external_calls.purpose: Prepare/assign process/object attribution using the context actor/item (BP+06h) so the following bark call is run/credited under the correct info.
Adjust process exclusion/visibility state before emitting the bark, likely to prevent re-entrance or to change how the bark is routed.
Deliver the literal message 'lamp ' to be shown to the player/actor identified by the context parameter; returns a value consumed by an implication operation.
Release the temporary string buffer allocated for the bark message after Item::bark returns (inferred from cleanup opcodes).
Terminate the handler, yielding control back to the engine (final operation).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: When the object receives a 'U8P_EWLAMPTP::look' event, it prepares process attribution/state, constructs the literal text "lamp ", and invokes the item's display/bark routine so the player sees the word "lamp".
